summary: This brief post shares a reader's tip for removing glue from woodworking glueups. Jacques Richer suggests using a cabinet scraper as a superior alternative to chiseling or wiping. The technique involves waiting about 90 minutes for the glue to reach a semi-solid state, then scraping it off cleanly. It's presented as a practical update to an earlier article on the same topic.
key_points: A cabinet scraper offers a cleaner alternative to chiseling or wiping for glue removal
Timing matters: waiting ~90 minutes lets glue reach an ideal semi-solid state for scraping
The method avoids both the chipping of chisel removal and the mess of wiping
money_quotes: Scrape with a cabinet scraper – wiping it fairly often. It doesn't chip like using a chisel, and it doesn't take nearly as long or make nearly as much mess as wiping.
key_insight: A cabinet scraper applied to semi-cured glue offers the cleanest and most efficient method for removing squeeze-out from woodworking glueups.
